drugs.notes: 45mg once daily days 1-28 inclusive. Available as 15mg and 45mg film-coated tablets. Swallow whole, do not crush or dissolve. May be taken with or without food. Dose reductions to 30mg or 15mg as per toxicity guidelines.

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until neutrophils recover to >=1.5x10^9/L, then resume at 45mg
Withhold until neutrophils recover to >=1.5x10^9/L, then resume at 30mg
Withhold until neutrophils recover to >=1.5x10^9/L, then resume at 15mg
Withhold until platelets recover to >=75x10^9/L, then resume at 45mg
Withhold until platelets recover to >=75x10^9/L, then resume at 30mg
Withhold until platelets recover to >=75x10^9/L, then resume at 15mg

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: AST/ALT Grade 3 (>5xULN) or Grade 2 (>3-5xULN) for >7 days - previously on 45mg
dose_modifications.notes: Stop, monitor LFTs. Once AST/ALT <=3xULN resume at 30mg

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: AST/ALT Grade 3 (>5xULN) or Grade 2 (>3-5xULN) for >7 days - previously on 30mg
dose_modifications.notes: Stop, monitor LFTs. Once AST/ALT <=3xULN resume at 15mg
Renal excretion not major route. CrCl >=50ml/min: no adjustment. CrCl <50ml/min or ESRD: use with caution - not studied in this cohort

dose_modifications.condition: Pancreatitis: Grade 2 or asymptomatic lipase/amylase elevation <=2xULN

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold, resume at 30mg after recovery to <=grade 1 (<1.5xULN)
Withhold, resume at 15mg after recovery to <=grade 1 (<1.5xULN)
Withhold, resume at 30mg after recovery to <Grade 2
Withhold, resume at 15mg after recovery to <Grade 2

special_warnings: Arterial and venous thrombosis/occlusion risk - major vascular events warning
QTc prolongation risk
Pancreatitis risk - acute pancreatitis more frequent in first two months; monitor serum lipase/amylase every 2 weeks for first 2 months
FBC, U&Es and LFTs every 2 weeks for first 3 months then monthly
Check Hepatitis B, C and HIV status prior to starting therapy
HBV carriers and those with active disease must be discussed with a consultant hepatologist
Application for funding required before prescribing - not all licensed indications are funded
Must only be prescribed under supervision of a consultant haematologist
Ponatinib interacts with many other agents - always check for drug interactions
Can cause drowsiness - advise patient not to drive or use machinery if affected
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed

protocol_name: Chronic Lymphocytic Leukaemia - Rituximab Fludarabine Cyclophosphamide (IV Cyclophosphamide)

drugs.notes: Cycle 1 only: 375mg/m² in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%, starting at 50mg/hour, increasing by 50mg/hour every 30 minutes to maximum 400mg/hour. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg (up if halfway). | CYCLE 1 DOSE (375mg/m²): two Rituximab entries exist — 375mg/m² for cycle 1 only (standard CLL lower first-dose) and 500mg/m² for cycles 2 onwards. Both are intentional and represent different cycle doses.

Cycles 2 onwards: 500mg/m² in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% as per local rituximab infusion guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg (up if halfway). [VERIFIED: Rituximab 500mg/m² from cycle 2 onwards in CLL is standard per MHRA SmPC and NICE TA174 — higher than lymphoma dosing of 375mg/m²] | CYCLES 2+ DOSE (500mg/m²): two Rituximab entries exist — 375mg/m² for cycle 1 only and 500mg/m² for cycles 2 onwards. Both are intentional and represent different cycle doses.
All cycles: 250mg/m² IV bolus days 1, 2, 3. Dose banded according to agreed local bands (up if halfway).
All cycles: 25mg/m² in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes, days 1, 2, 3. Dose banded according to national dose bands (up if halfway).

dose_modifications.notes: Delay 7 days; if counts recover give 100%. If 14 days needed to recover, restart at 50% dose reduction
If 14 days required for counts to recover then restart with 50% dose reduction
Delay 7 days; if counts recover give 100%. If 14 days needed to recover, restart at 50% dose reduction
If 14 days required for counts to recover then restart with 50% dose reduction

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >21 µmol/L or AST/ALT 2-3xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Clinical decision. Evidence that exposure to active metabolites may not be increased, suggesting dose reduction may not be necessary
Delay until adverse effect resolves to grade <=2, then reduce to 75%. If recurs reduce to 50% or discontinue

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (30 minutes prior to rituximab)
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV (30 minutes prior to rituximab)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to rituximab)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (on days of chemotherapy)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (take home)
Ondansetron 8mg oral twice a day for 5 days starting evening of day 1 (take home)
Allopurinol 300mg oral once a day for 7 days (cycle 1 only)
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice a day for 28 days
Co-trimoxazole 960mg oral once a day Monday, Wednesday, Friday for 28 days
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for rituximab infusion reactions
Salbutamol 2.5mg nebule when required for rituximab-related bronchospasm
special_warnings: Fludarabine: lifelong risk of transfusion-associated graft versus host disease (TA-GVHD) - IRRADIATED BLOOD PRODUCTS required for life
Blood transfusion departments must be notified immediately when decision to treat is made
Patient must be issued with an alert card to carry at all times
Rituximab: severe cytokine release syndrome risk - typically within 1-2 hours of first infusion
Rituximab: progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) risk - monitor for neurological/cognitive/psychiatric symptoms
Rituximab: hypersensitivity/anaphylaxis risk - have resuscitation equipment available
Hepatitis B status must be checked prior to starting rituximab
FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 of each cycle
If PML suspected, suspend dosing until excluded; if confirmed, permanently discontinue rituximab
If third dose reduction necessary, treatment should be stopped

protocol_name: Chronic Lymphocytic Leukaemia - Rituximab Fludarabine Cyclophosphamide (IV Cyclophosphamide, Split Rituximab)

drugs.notes: Cycle 1 only, Day 1: 100mg flat dose in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 120 minutes. Split dose indicated where lymphocytes >25x10^9/L.
Cycle 1 only, Day 2: 325mg/m² in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%, starting at 50mg/hour, increasing by 50mg/hour every 30 minutes to maximum 400mg/hour. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg (up if halfway).

Cycles 2 onwards: 500mg/m² in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% as per local rituximab infusion guidelines. If lymphocytes >25x10^9/L on day 1, consider fractionating: Day 1 125mg/m² in 100ml NaCl 0.9%, Day 2 375mg/m² in 500ml NaCl 0.9%. [VERIFIED: Rituximab 500mg/m² from cycle 2 onwards in CLL is standard per MHRA SmPC and NICE TA174 — higher than lymphoma dosing of 375mg/m²]
All cycles: 250mg/m² IV bolus days 1, 2, 3. Dose banded according to agreed local bands (up if halfway).
All cycles: 25mg/m² in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes, days 1, 2, 3. Dose banded according to national dose bands (up if halfway).

Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to rituximab)

Fludarabine: lifelong risk of TA-GVHD - IRRADIATED BLOOD PRODUCTS required for life
Blood transfusion departments must be notified immediately when decision to treat is made
Patient must be issued with an alert card at all times
Rituximab: severe cytokine release syndrome risk
Rituximab: PML risk - monitor for neurological/cognitive/psychiatric symptoms

ORAL CHEMOTHERAPY — NPSA/2008/RRR001: Patient must be counselled and provided written information before starting. Prescriptions must be written on an approved oral chemotherapy prescription form. Dispensing must only be by a trained pharmacist. Patient must be provided with a chemotherapy alert card. Do not supply more than one cycle at a time without clinical review.

drugs.notes: Dose varies by indication and platelet count. Idiopathic MF starting dose: Platelets 50-99x10^9/L = 5mg BD; Platelets 100-200x10^9/L = 15mg BD; Platelets >200x10^9/L = 20mg BD. Post-PV MF: 10mg BD. Maximum dose 25mg BD. Twice daily dosing. Take at same time each day with or without food. Protocol set at lowest starting dose 5mg BD in ARIA - must be adjusted per platelet count and clinical assessment.

dose_modifications.notes: Starting dose 10mg twice daily regardless of platelet count
Consider dose reduction; ideally avoid treatment interruptions for thrombocytopenia
Recommended starting dose based on platelet count should be reduced by approximately 50%, to be administered twice a day. Monitor CBC with differential at least every 1-2 weeks for first 6 weeks
Recommended starting dose (based on platelet count) should be reduced by 50%

dose_modifications.condition: Co-administration of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or dual CYP2C9/CYP3A4 inhibitors (e.g. fluconazole)
dose_modifications.notes: Unit dose should be reduced by approximately 50%, administered twice a day. More frequent monitoring recommended. Avoid if possible.

special_warnings: JAK1/2 inhibitor - dose selection MUST be based on platelet count prior to starting
Haemoglobin nearly always falls initially before plateau at ~6 weeks - consider erythropoietin if symptomatic or Hb <8g/dL
If patient becomes transfusion dependent, review suitability of ruxolitinib continuation

Substantially increased risk of Herpes Zoster (HZV) reactivation - check HZV status and titres before starting; seropositive patients commence aciclovir minimum 400mg BD (increase to 800mg TDS if reactivation occurs)
Progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) reported - monitor for cognitive/neurological/psychiatric symptoms
Non-melanoma skin cancers (NMSCs) reported - periodic skin examination recommended
Lipid abnormalities - lipid monitoring and treatment of dyslipidaemia recommended
Screen for HIV and HepB (HepBsAg and HepBcAb) before starting
Screen for active infections; do not start until active serious infections resolved
Consider screening for latent TB or herpes zoster
Gradual tapering recommended on discontinuation; avoid abrupt cessation where possible
Starting dose should not be increased within first 4 weeks; thereafter no more frequently than every 2 weeks
Maximum dose 25mg twice daily
If dose missed, patient should take next scheduled dose - do not double dose
FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to starting and every 2-4 weeks until dose stabilised, then every 2-3 months
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed

protocol_name: ALL Tisagenlecleucel (Tisacel) with Lymphodepletion Cyclophosphamide 500mg/m² and Fludarabine 30mg/m²

drugs.notes: Lymphodepletion: 30mg/m² in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes on days -6, -5, -4, -3. Dose banded according to national dose band (25mg/ml). Lymphodepleting regimen must only be started after availability of Tisacel is confirmed.

Lymphodepletion: 500mg/m² IV bolus over 10 minutes on days -6 and -5. Dose banded in accordance with national dose banding table (20mg/ml). Lymphodepleting chemotherapy may be omitted if WBC <1.0x10^9 cells/L within one week prior to infusion.

Target dose: Patients <=50kg: 0.2 to 5x10^6 cells/kg body weight. Patients >50kg: 0.1 to 2.5x10^8 cells (non-weight based). Total range 1.2x10^6 to 6x10^8 cells. Administer on Day 0 (CAR-T infusion day). Infuse within 30 minutes of thaw completion over maximum 30 minutes. Administer gravimetrically (NOT via volumetric pump). Use latex-free giving set WITHOUT leukocyte-depleting filter, primed with sodium chloride 0.9%. For autologous use only.

dose_modifications.notes: High dose therapy not generally undertaken below CrCl 30ml/min - clinical decision
Contraindicated - do not use fludarabine. Consider alternative lymphodepletion regimen (cytarabine/etoposide).
No dose modification recommended for hepatic dysfunction
Severe hepatic impairment may decrease activation of cyclophosphamide - consider when selecting dose and interpreting response

dose_modifications.condition: Significant cytopenia: WBC <1.0x10^9 cells/L within 1 week prior to infusion

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (30 minutes prior to Tisacel on Day 0)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to Tisacel on Day 0)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV three times a day
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV twice a day
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice a day (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Fluconazole 100mg oral once a day (antifungal prophylaxis)
Pentamidine 300mg nebuliser during lymphodepletion - continue every 28 days until count recovery for co-trimoxazole use
Posaconazole 300mg once daily if prolonged neutropenia or previous invasive fungal infection
Gastric protection (PPI or H2 antagonist) from day 1 of lymphodepletion until platelets >50x10^9/L
Levetiracetam 500mg twice daily oral from day 0 until day +30 (seizure prophylaxis - consider due to neurotoxicity risk)
Tocilizumab 8mg/kg (maximum 800mg) IV 8-hourly if required for CRS - maximum 4 doses; 4 doses must be available on ward prior to Tisacel infusion
Allopurinol 300mg oral once a day OR Rasburicase 7.5mg IV once a day for TLS prophylaxis
special_warnings: CAR-T THERAPY - FOR AUTOLOGOUS USE ONLY
Only available at the Wessex Blood and Marrow Transplant Unit
CAR-T cell therapy: single administration procedure. cycle_length_days is not applicable — this is a one-time infusion following lymphodepletion conditioning.

Cytokine Release Syndrome (CRS): nearly all patients experience some degree of CRS including life-threatening and fatal reactions. Symptoms: pyrexia, tachycardia, dyspnoea, hypoxia, capillary leak, hypotension. Monitor vitals 4-hourly on Day 0, then twice daily.
ICANS (Immune Effector Cell-Associated Neurotoxicity Syndrome): seizures, somnolence, headaches, confusion, encephalopathy, speech disorders - ICE score assessed twice daily
CRS or ICANS symptoms can occur WEEKS after infusion - patient must carry alert card at all times
Fludarabine: lifelong risk of TA-GVHD - IRRADIATED BLOOD PRODUCTS required for life
Prophylactic systemic corticosteroids are NOT recommended - may interfere with CAR-T activity

If patient had previous Grade 4 haemorrhagic cystitis with cyclophosphamide or chemo-refractory state to cyclophosphamide-containing regimen: use alternative cytarabine/etoposide lymphodepletion regimen
Lymphodepleting regimen must only be started after Tisacel availability is confirmed
CAR-T administration must not occur out of core hours or over a weekend
Screening for HBV, HCV and HIV required before cell collection for manufacture
Echocardiogram and baseline lung function required prior to lymphodepletion
Do NOT administer via volumetric pump - administer gravimetrically
Do NOT use leukocyte-depleting filter
Administer within 30 minutes of thaw completion
Report all suspected adverse reactions to CAR-T infusion via MHRA Yellow Card

protocol_name: ALL Tisagenlecleucel (Tisacel) with Lymphodepletion Cytarabine 500mg/m² and Etoposide 150mg/m²

drugs.notes: Lymphodepletion: 500mg/m² in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes on days -5 and -4. Dose banded according to national dose bands (100mg/ml). Lymphodepleting regimen must only be started after availability of Tisacel is confirmed.

Lymphodepletion: 150mg/m² in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes on days -5, -4, -3. Dose banded according to national dose bands. Lymphodepleting chemotherapy may be omitted if WBC <=1000 cells/µL within one week prior to infusion.

Target dose: Patients <=50kg: 0.2 to 5x10^6 cells/kg body weight. Patients >50kg: 0.1 to 2.5x10^8 cells (non-weight based). Total range 1.2x10^6 to 6x10^8 cells. Infuse on Day 0 within 30 minutes of thaw completion, over maximum 30 minutes. Administer gravimetrically (NOT via volumetric pump). Use latex-free giving set WITHOUT leukocyte-depleting filter, primed with sodium chloride 0.9%. For autologous use only.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units/L
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >51 µmol/L or AST/ALT >180 units/L
dose_modifications.notes: Clinical decision. Consider reducing dose if significant reductions in albumin levels occur.

dose_modifications.condition: Significant cytopenia: WBC <=1000 cells/µL within 1 week prior to infusion

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (30 minutes prior to Tisacel on Day 0)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 minutes prior to Tisacel on Day 0)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV three times a day
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV twice a day
Prednisolone 0.5% eye drops into each eye four times a day - continue for 5 days after cytarabine administration
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice a day (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Fluconazole 100mg oral once a day (antifungal prophylaxis)
Pentamidine 300mg nebuliser during lymphodepletion - continue every 28 days until count recovery for co-trimoxazole use
Posaconazole 300mg once daily if prolonged neutropenia or previous invasive fungal infection
Gastric protection (PPI or H2 antagonist) from day 1 of lymphodepletion until platelets >50x10^9/L
Levetiracetam 500mg twice daily oral from day 0 until day +30 (seizure prophylaxis)
Tocilizumab 8mg/kg (maximum 800mg) IV 8-hourly if required for CRS - maximum 4 doses; 4 doses must be available on ward prior to Tisacel infusion
Allopurinol 300mg oral once a day OR Rasburicase 7.5mg IV once a day for TLS prophylaxis
special_warnings: CAR-T THERAPY - FOR AUTOLOGOUS USE ONLY
Only available at the Wessex Blood and Marrow Transplant Unit
CAR-T cell therapy: single administration procedure. cycle_length_days is not applicable — this is a one-time infusion following lymphodepletion conditioning.

Alternative lymphodepletion regimen to Cy/Flu: use when patient had previous Grade 4 haemorrhagic cystitis with cyclophosphamide OR demonstrated chemo-refractory state to cyclophosphamide-containing regimen

Cytokine Release Syndrome (CRS): nearly all patients experience some degree of CRS including life-threatening and fatal reactions. Symptoms: pyrexia, tachycardia, dyspnoea, hypoxia, capillary leak, hypotension. Monitor vitals 4-hourly on Day 0, then twice daily.
ICANS (Immune Effector Cell-Associated Neurotoxicity Syndrome): seizures, somnolence, headaches, confusion, encephalopathy, speech disorders - ICE score assessed twice daily
CRS or ICANS symptoms can occur WEEKS after infusion - patient must carry alert card at all times
Prophylactic systemic corticosteroids are NOT recommended - may interfere with CAR-T activity. May be used for CRS/ICANS treatment under consultant advice.
Lymphodepleting regimen must only be started after Tisacel availability is confirmed
CAR-T administration must not occur out of core hours or over a weekend
Screening for HBV, HCV and HIV required before cell collection for manufacture
Echocardiogram and baseline lung function required prior to lymphodepletion
Prednisolone eye drops required due to cytarabine ocular toxicity risk
Do NOT administer via volumetric pump - administer gravimetrically
Do NOT use leukocyte-depleting filter
Administer within 30 minutes of thaw completion
Report all suspected adverse reactions to CAR-T infusion via MHRA Yellow Card
Etoposide: anaphylactic reactions risk; consider albumin levels when dosing
